vulnerability.title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Ignore ICMP Broadcast Echo Requests on IPv4 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list

triage_decision.reason: Setting net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1 is a standard networking hardening change that does not affect authentication, boot, or critical services. It can be applied immediately via sysctl and made persistent without reboot.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to output JSON with the commands to fix the issue. The finding: Enable Kernel Parameter to Ignore ICMP Broadcast Echo Requests on IPv4 Interfaces. So we need to set kernel parameter net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1.

In Rocky Linux (RHEL based), kernel parameters are set via sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1 and to persist across reboots add a file in /etc/sysctl.d/ with content "net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1".

Thus we need two commands: one to set runtime, another to create persistence file.

We must use run_cmd for exactly one shell command at a time. We cannot chain. So we need two separate invocations: first run_cmd to set sysctl, second run_cmd to create file.

We also need to use read_file to inspect target config file before modifying? The instructions: "ALWAYS use read_file to inspect the target config file BEFORE modifying it." So before writing the file, we should read it to see if exists. We'll do read_file on /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.conf (or similar). Then write_file.

Thus sequence:

1. run_cmd: sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1
2. read_file: path = /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.conf
3. write_file: path = /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.conf, content = "net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1\n", mode = "0644" (or appropriate). We'll just set mode "0644".

We must output JSON array of tool calls. Each tool call is an object with function and parameters.

We need to output something like:

[
  {"function":"run_cmd","parameter":{"command":"sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1"}},
  {"function":"read_file","parameter":{"path":"/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.conf"}},
  {"function":"write_file","parameter":{"path":"/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts.conf","content":"net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1\n","mode":"0644"}}
]

Check if any other constraints: Avoid referencing $... etc. We didn't.

Thus final answer.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to remediate the OpenSCAP finding: set net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1, both runtime and persistently.

We must first check current value via sysctl, then set it, and ensure a file in /etc/sysctl.d/ contains the line.

We must use only run_cmd, read_file, write_file. One command at a time.

Plan:
1. Check current value: run_cmd with sysctl net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts
2. If not 1, set via sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts=1
3. Ensure persistence: check /etc/sysctl.d/ for existing config; we can write a file e.g., /etc/sysctl.d/99-ICMP_ignore_broadcasts.conf with line net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1
But we must first read if there's already such line to avoid duplicate. But we can just overwrite or create.

We need to follow the process: first call review_plan with plan description. Then after approval, execute.

Thus we need to output a call to review_plan with plan_description. Then later we will get approval? Actually we need to wait for approval before applying. So we just output the review_plan call.

Thus final answer: call review_plan with description.
We need to produce JSON with the fields. The system gave example. We'll output similar.
